built: designer-31 F1-K kernel-v1 PILOT explication batch ($0, benchmark-blind)
selection_rule: P1(disjoint_m8 & header-clean) -> genus prefilter R -> stratify AGENTIVE/ACT/STATE -> URN byte order -> round-robin to 15

records.label: lover
records.bar_note: clean genus-differentia; WN gloss was circular (lover/loves) and disjunctive, so authored fresh.
records.gloss: Someone who feels deep affection or romantic love toward another person, or who is the object of such love; especially a person with whom one shares an intimate romantic attachment.
records.wn_gloss: a person who loves someone or is loved by someone

records.label: appearance
records.bar_note: WN gloss ('the act of appearing in public view') is circular; authored fresh around the seeable-before/after core.
records.gloss: The event of someone or something coming to be seen, especially by coming before other people in a public place; a becoming-present to view.
records.wn_gloss: the act of appearing in public view; "the rookie made a brief appearance in the first period"; "it was Bernhardt's last appearance in America"

records.label: cheerfulness
records.bar_note: WN gloss ('being cheerful and dispelling gloom') is circular; authored fresh around felt-gladness that spreads to others.
records.gloss: A disposition marked by good spirits and evident gladness, such that one readily feels contentment and lifts the mood of those nearby.
records.wn_gloss: the quality of being cheerful and dispelling gloom; "flowers added a note of cheerfulness to the drab room"

records.label: peer (X is a peer of Y)
records.bar_note: WN core ('of equal standing with another in a group') is a clean genus-differentia; expanded from 11 words to meet the length convention and to make 'equal standing' explicit.
records.gloss: A person who holds the same rank, standing, or status as another within a group, being neither above nor below them in position or authority.
records.wn_gloss: a person who is of equal standing with another in a group

records.label: apparition
records.bar_note: genuinely distinct from 'appearance' only by the sudden/unexpected differentia; deliberately near-synonymous to probe whether the reviewer discriminates the two senses.
records.gloss: An occasion on which something comes suddenly into view when no one expected to see it, so that those present are taken by surprise; a sudden, unlooked-for becoming-visible.
records.wn_gloss: an act of appearing or becoming visible unexpectedly; "natives were amazed at the apparition of this white stranger"

records.label: changelessness
records.bar_note: WN gloss ('being unchangeable') is circular; authored fresh, but 'change' is not among the primes, so both prose and AST lean on the SAME/OTHER contrast — the reviewer should check that this is faithful, not merely available.
records.gloss: The quality of remaining the same through time, such that a thing stays as it is and does not come to be other than it was.
records.wn_gloss: the quality of being unchangeable; having a marked tendency to remain unchanged

records.label: wrongdoer
records.bar_note: WN core ('transgresses moral or civil law') is clean and non-circular; expanded for length and to separate the two forbidding sources (morality / law).
records.gloss: A person who does what moral standards or the law forbid; one who commits an offence, wrong, or misdeed against others or against established rules.
records.wn_gloss: a person who transgresses moral or civil law

records.label: exit
records.bar_note: WN gloss ('the act of going out') is non-circular but too terse; expanded to state the inside-to-outside movement.
records.gloss: The act of going out of a place; a movement by which someone or something passes from inside an enclosure or space to a position outside it.

records.label: fidelity
records.bar_note: WN gloss ('being faithful') is circular and one word of content; authored fresh around keeping-one's-word plus not-betraying.
records.gloss: Steadfast faithfulness in keeping one's promises and obligations to another, so that one continues to act as one has undertaken and does not betray the trust placed in one.

records.label: artist's model
records.bar_note: WN core ('poses for a painter or sculptor') is clean; expanded to state the defining condition (holding still to be depicted).
records.gloss: A person who poses, keeping still in a chosen attitude, so that a painter or sculptor can observe them and make a picture or figure of them.
records.wn_gloss: a person who poses for a painter or sculptor

records.label: ransom
records.bar_note: the WN synset gloss ('the act of freeing from captivity or punishment') OMITS the defining payment; authoring it in restores the true intension but arguably narrows the synset — an alignment call the human reviewer must make.
records.gloss: The act of securing the release of a captive by paying the price demanded for it; also the sum so paid, or the freeing thereby obtained.
records.wn_gloss: the act of freeing from captivity or punishment

records.label: continuousness
records.bar_note: WN gloss ('something that continues without end or interruption') is circular; authored fresh around unbroken persistence.
records.gloss: The quality of a thing that goes on without break or pause, continuing as one unbroken whole through time rather than stopping and starting.
records.wn_gloss: the quality of something that continues without end or interruption

records.label: bill poster
records.bar_note: clean WN gloss, but the AST cannot carry 'printed notices / words', so the reviewer must judge the prose alone; a reviewer expecting AST-prose parity may fail it.
records.gloss: Someone whose work is to fix printed notices, posters, or placards onto walls, boards, and other public surfaces, so that many people will see them.
records.wn_gloss: someone who pastes up bills or placards on walls or billboards

records.label: throw
records.bar_note: WN parenthetical ('propelling something with a rapid movement of the arm and wrist') is the real definition; led with it and dropped the circular 'the act of throwing'.
records.gloss: The act of sending something through the air by a rapid movement of the arm and hand, releasing it so that it travels away from the thrower.
records.wn_gloss: the act of throwing (propelling something with a rapid movement of the arm and wrist); "the catcher made a good throw to second base"

records.label: wealth (profuse abundance)
records.bar_note: this is the ABUNDANCE sense of the eligible synset (n-05123428), NOT the riches/money sense a reviewer may expect; the gloss must make the sense unmistakable or the WN alignment check will read as the wrong sense.
records.gloss: A great abundance of some valued thing, such that there is far more of it present than is usual or than could be needed.
records.wn_gloss: the quality of profuse abundance; "she has a wealth of talent"
